pi_items.source: verifier.py line 56 defines required_labels as only 6 items; metadata claims 11. Task description mentions all 11 players but verifier only enforces 6.

Verifier (lines 92-131) checks for line styles (curved/straight/dashed) but never validates specific route names. Metadata claims these names are required, but the verifier code contains no checks for 'banana', 'flat', 'shallow', or 'post' strings.
setup_task.sh line 12, task.json description, verified via web search as real NFL play popularized by Jon Gruden
setup_task.sh line 13, task.json description, verified via web search as real West Coast Offense formation used with Spider 2 Y Banana
setup_task.sh: play_call_sheet.txt created via heredoc (lines 11-37), playbook_template.drawio created via heredoc (lines 41-99). No external URLs or file downloads.
verifier.py lines 98-131: checks curved_routes, straight_routes, dashed_routes counts but never parses route names

privileged_info_summary: This task requires digitizing an American football playbook diagram in draw.io. The play is 'Spider 2 Y Banana' (a real NFL play) from 'Green Right Strong' formation. The verifier checks for 6 specific player labels (QB, C, Y, Z, X, FB) and requires curved/straight/dashed route lines, but does NOT verify specific route names despite metadata claiming they are required. All data (play call sheet and template diagram) is synthetic, generated fresh by the task setup script. The expected output is spider_2_y_banana.pdf. A critical discrepancy exists: metadata claims 11 required labels, but the verifier only checks for 6, making HB, LT, LG, RG, RT optional despite being mentioned in the task description.
